claim: Researchers detected cocaine in multiple, freshwater-shrimp samples across the U.K.

result.claim: Researchers detected cocaine in multiple, freshwater-shrimp samples across the U.K.
result.reasoning: The knowledge base does not contain any direct evidence to support the claim that researchers detected cocaine in multiple freshwater shrimp samples across the U.K. Searches for "cocaine detected in freshwater shrimp samples U.K.", "drugs in freshwater shrimp U.K.", "cocaine in freshwater shrimp U.K.", and "drugs in freshwater shrimp" returned propositions and chunks related to antidepressants in fish, mercury in tuna, or general drug testing, but none specifically mentioned cocaine in freshwater shrimp in the U.K.

evidence.text: Atlantic bluefin tuna (ABFT, Thunnus thynnus) are apex predators in marine food webs.
evidence.source: Declining Mercury Concentrations in Bluefin Tuna Reflect Reduced Emissions to the North Atlantic Ocean

evidence.text: Anatomical alterations were noted within the testes of fish exposed to FLX.

evidence.text: Atlantic bluefin tuna (ABFT) Hg concentrations ranged from 0.25 to 3.15 mg kg–1.

evidence.text: Atlantic bluefin tuna (ABFT) provide ∼40% of mercury (Hg) to the U.S. population.
evidence.source: Declining Mercury Concentrations in Bluefin Tuna Reflect Reduced Emissions to the North Atlantic Ocean

evidence.text: Bupropion (BUP), fluoxetine (FLX), sertraline (SER), and venlafaxine (VEN) are antidepressant pharmaceuticals found in wastewater effluent.
evidence.source: Selective uptake and biological consequences of environmentally relevant antidepressant pharmaceutical exposures on male fathead minnows
